verdict: ACCEPTED AS BENCHCORE BENCHMARK PACK FOR AGENT_X UNIVERSAL-AGENT FOUNDATION HARDENING

completion_criteria.criterion: Per-PDF semantic coverage exists for every PDF
Visual inventory exists for all requirement-bearing visuals
All accepted requirements have source, artifact, and tests
Product-specific BenchCore concepts bounded or abstracted
Learning policy blocks unsafe training claims
Operations documents are deferred boundaries only
Sabotage tests pass by failing unsafe behavior
Clean replay works from fresh checkout/copy
Final report contains no forbidden universal-agent claim
Final verdict is honest and bounded

anti_overclaim_gate.check: Does the benchmark cover all 32 BenchCore PDFs?
Does every PDF have accepted/deferred/optional/rejected handling?
Are visuals and diagrams accounted for?
Are BenchCore-specific product details prevented from entering Agent_X core?
Are generic reusable Agent_X patterns extracted?
Are positive, negative, and sabotage tests present?
Can a clean checkout replay the benchmark build?
Does the final report avoid claiming instant universality?

forbidden_claims_not_made: Agent_X can now build any agent safely
Agent_X can now operate live MOS, OverDrive, Inception, customer databases, SSH log tailing, WSL, or production systems
One project benchmark proves general intelligence or universal agency
allowed_claim_used: Agent_X has integrated BenchCore as a real-world benchmark pack that strengthens its universal-agent foundation.
